title: Best VPNs for Travel in 2026: Stay Safe on the Road
description: The best VPNs for travelers. Fast, reliable, and unblocked in 195+ countries. How to choose a VPN for your next trip.
content:
Traveling to a new country means using unfamiliar WiFi networks. Coffee shops, hotels, airports. Every network is a potential security risk. A VPN encrypts your data, hides your IP address, and makes it impossible for the network to see what you're doing. But not all VPNs are created equal for travel. You need speed (because slow VPN is worse than no VPN), reliability (because a dropped connection while abroad is a disaster), and coverage in every country you're visiting.

ExpressVPN is the gold standard for travelers. 150+ server locations in 94 countries. Speeds are genuinely fast even on slow hotel WiFi. The app is available on iOS, Android, Mac, Windows, and routers. One of the few VPNs that works reliably in China. Has a 30-day money-back guarantee, which means you can test it for your next trip risk-free. Cost: 6.67 dollars/month (annual) or 12.95 dollars/month (monthly).

Surfshark is the budget traveler's choice. 3,200 servers in 100 countries. Works in most places, though not quite as reliable in restrictive countries as ExpressVPN. The app is lightweight and doesn't drain your battery as much as competitors. Unlimited simultaneous connections (you can cover your whole family). Cost: 2.19 dollars/month (annual) or 12.99 dollars/month (monthly). This is the best value.

NordVPN has 6,000 servers in 111 countries. The speed is solid. NordVPN is popular with travelers because it has dedicated servers for streaming (Netflix, Disney+) which work better than competitor VPNs when you're abroad. Two simultaneous connections on the standard plan. Cost: 3.99 dollars/month (annual) or 11.99 dollars/month (monthly).

ProtonVPN is owned by the Swiss company Proton, which has a strong privacy reputation. No-logging policy is audited by independent third parties. Solid in 118 countries. Free plan available (limited countries, slower speeds) which is useful for testing before you buy. Paid plan is 9.99 dollars/month (annual). Good option if privacy is your top concern.

CyberGhost has 11,500 servers in 100 countries, the highest server count of any VPN. This means faster speeds in most locations because there are more servers to distribute load. Special streaming profiles for different services. Cost: 2.19 dollars/month (annual) or 12.99 dollars/month (monthly). Good for speed.

What you need to know when choosing a VPN for travel: First, check if the VPN works in your destination country. China, UAE, Russia, and Turkey block most VPNs. ExpressVPN and Surfshark generally work in these countries, but confirmation online before your trip is essential. Second, speed matters. Download the VPN's app before you travel. Test it on your home WiFi to make sure it doesn't cut your speeds in half. Third, download the app and set it up before you leave home. Struggling to install a VPN app on foreign WiFi is a headache. Fourth, buy a one-month or three-month plan, not annual, until you're sure the VPN works for you.

Pro traveler setup: Use ExpressVPN for the absolute most reliable protection, especially if traveling to restrictive countries. Use Surfshark if you're budget-conscious and traveling in Western Europe or North America. Enable kill switch in the app settings. A kill switch cuts off your internet connection if the VPN drops, preventing accidental data leaks. Connect to a server in your home country, not the country you're visiting. This way, websites think you're at home and won't block you. Use your VPN even on hotel WiFi that looks secure. Hotel WiFi is scanned constantly by hackers. Use it for email, banking, and sensitive work. Turn on auto-connect so your VPN reconnects automatically if you move between WiFi networks.

A few final considerations: Battery drain is real. VPNs drain your phone battery 15 to 30% faster than normal. This matters when you're exploring all day. ExpressVPN and Surfshark drain the least battery. Streaming is slower on VPN. If you're trying to watch Netflix from your home country, accept that it will buffer slightly more. Some VPNs optimize for this better than others. NordVPN and CyberGhost have streaming-specific servers that maintain decent speeds. Payment might be difficult abroad if your card gets declined. Buy your VPN subscription before you travel. Finally, VPN usage is restricted or monitored in some countries. Traveling through China, UAE, Russia, or Turkey? Check current regulations before your trip. VPN use itself is usually legal, but how you use it matters.
